channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The FBI Warns Against a Dangerous Ransomware Scheme - Cyber Threats on the Rise
item.description: The FBI and US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency warn against a ransomware scheme using Medusa software, affecting hundreds of people worldwide.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Angry Americans are hitting Trump where it hurts the most: Elon Musk
item.description: Earlier this week, Donald Trump staged what historians believe to be a presidential first: a stilted, fumbling White House press conference convened to boost the fortunes of a valuable donor’s floundering car company.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Over 90% of Americans demand a "right-to-disconnect" law which would protect them from out-of-hours work communication
item.description: Work email overload is causing significant stress for American workers, with many feeling pressured to respond quickly to irrelevant work emails, even outside of office hours.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: M4 MacBook Air 2025 review: The perfect laptop for most consumers
item.description: Apple’s new MacBook Air laptops, which the company unveiled alongside a new iPad Air and a disappointing 11th-generation iPad earlier this month, are now on sale. They feature the M4 chip and some additional upgrades that remote workers and desk jockeys, in particular, may appreciate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: NASA Astronauts Get Relief Crew, Ready to Return
item.description: NASA's Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams get new crew on SpaceX, paving way for their return after nine long months.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: JDK 25: The new features in Java 25
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 25, a planned long-term support release of standard Java due in September, now has two features officially proposed for it. The first is a preview of an API for stable values, a feature that promises to speed up the startup of Java applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tech stocks tumble as AI boom fizzles out
item.description: The world’s biggest technology companies have seen their stock prices tumble over the past month as wider tumult in the market hits the tech sector hard after years of steep gains driven by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: acebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI calls for US to centralize AI regulation
item.description: OpenAI executives think the federal government should regulate artificial intelligence in the US, taking precedence over often more restrictive state regulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This volunteer crew is on a mission to fix Wikipedia’s worst celebrity photos
item.description: Visit a celebrity’s Wikipedia page and there’s a good chance you’ll be greeted by a blurry, outdated, or unflattering photo. These images often look like they were snapped in passing at a public event—because, in many cases, they were.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: An inside look at Las Vegas valley’s first AI-powered hotel
item.description: Otonomous Hotel, under construction at Russell and Decatur, uses machine learning to create a personalized experience for guests.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, NewsNation

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google unveils Gemma 3 multi-modal AI models
item.description: Google DeepMind has introduced Gemma 3, an update to the company’s family of generative AI models, featuring multi-modality that allows the models to analyze images, answer questions about images, identify objects, and perform other tasks that involve analyzing and understanding visual data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wyoming billionaire 1 of 4 in race to buy TikTok
item.description: Wyoming entrepreneur Reid Raisner told "NewsNation Live" on Monday that the fate of TikTok is a matter of U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: How genomics and AI could help treat cancer more effectively
item.description: “These discoveries highlight how genetic profiling can play a crucial role in personalizing cancer care,” Ruishan Liu said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
